info.name: ShipKit API
info.description:
ShipKit — the open shipping toolkit, secured by Lifted Payments 3-D Secure.

Buy multi-carrier shipping labels (USPS, UPS, FedEx and more via EasyPost) and take card payment authenticated by 3-D Secure, so fraud-chargeback liability shifts to the issuer.

## Getting started
1. Set the collection variables `baseUrl` (your ShipKit host — `http://localhost:8080` self-hosted) and `apiKey` (a ShipKit `sk_live_…`/`sk_test_…` secret key, or a `pk_…` publishable key for the customer flow).
2. Every `/api/*` request is pre-authorized with the `ShipKit-Api-Key: {{apiKey}}` header at the collection level — `GET /api/health` and the HMAC-verified EasyPost webhook override it to no-auth.
3. Admin-gated routes (`/api/keys`, key management) also send `X-Session-ID: {{adminSessionId}}` — a verified admin SMS session.

## Money
All amounts — carrier rates and charges — are **strings** (e.g. `"8.42"`). Parse them as decimals; never float- or string-compare.

## More
Raw OpenAPI 3.1 spec at `GET {{baseUrl}}/openapi.yaml`; interactive reference at `GET {{baseUrl}}/docs`. Take live payments through a 3-D Secure merchant account at https://liftedholdings.com/payments.

Maintained by Daniel Wilson Kemp · Lifted Holdings.

item.name: Shipping
item.description: Carriers, rates, labels, bulk/international, and the EasyPost tracking webhook.

request.description: Liveness / readiness probe. No API key required. Reports which features are configured (`shipping`, `payments`).
Verify and normalize a delivery address (USPS delivery verification). `street1`, `city`, `state`, `zip` required; `country` defaults to `US`. Read back `residential` before comparing rates.

Create an EasyPost shipment and return carrier rates. `weight_oz` is OUNCES (required, > 0). Each `rate` is a STRING — sort as a decimal, select by explicit `id`. An empty `rates` with a populated `messages` is a carrier error.
Delivery-date-accurate SmartRates for an already-created shipment (EasyPost delivery-date percentiles).

Buy a label directly for a chosen rate when payment is handled out of band (no interactive 3-D Secure step). Secret-key only. `end_shipper_id` optional — the configured EndShipper is used when omitted.
Create and buy a batch of shipments in one call.
Generate a single SCAN form (manifest) for a batch so the carrier scans one barcode instead of every label.
Create customs info + items for an international shipment.
Read the configured EndShipper identity (the registered commercial shipper reused across purchases).
Register / update the EndShipper identity.
Fetch a stored label by its id. `404` when no label with that id exists.
Fetch the stored label for a payment session id — handy right after a purchase when you hold the `session_id` but not the label id.
Remove a label from ShipKit storage. This only deletes the local record — it does NOT void or refund the label with the carrier.

Receive EasyPost event webhooks. Authenticated by EasyPost's HMAC-SHA256 signature (NOT an API key) — send `X-Hmac-Signature: hmac-sha256-hex=` + the hex HMAC-SHA256 of the RAW body, keyed by the NFKD-normalized `EASYPOST_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Verified in constant time.

item.name: Payments
item.description: The Lifted Payments 3-D Secure payment flow: open a session, poll the server-verified status, then buy the label for an approved (liability-shifted) session.

request.description: Open a Lifted Payments 3-D Secure payment session for a chosen rate. The charge `amount` is computed on the SERVER (carrier rate + configured markup) — any client-sent amount is ignored. Returns `session_id`, the hosted 3-D Secure `form_url`, the server-computed `amount` (STRING), `currency`, and `expires_at`.

Poll the server-verified payment / 3-D Secure state. Status is confirmed against Lifted Payments — NEVER from return-URL params. `status` ∈ {pending, authenticated, approved, declined, failed}. A label may be bought ONLY when `status == "approved"` AND `three_ds.liability_shift == true`.

Re-verify the charge server-side, then buy the label. Idempotent — a second call returns the same label. Refuses (`402`) unless approved with a 3-D Secure liability shift; `410` when the session is past its charge window; `409` when a purchase is already in progress.

item.name: Keys
item.description: Admin-gated API-key management. Requires a valid API key (the `/api/*` gate) AND a verified admin session (`X-Session-ID: {{adminSessionId}}`).

request.description: Mint a new API key. `scope`: `secret` (`sk_…`, full server key, default) or `publishable` (`pk_…`, browser-safe, customer flow only). `mode`: `live` or `test`. The returned `api_key` is shown ONCE — store it immediately; only a SHA-256 hash is kept.
List API keys, newest first — metadata only (never the key or its hash).
Revoke an API key by id. Idempotent — revoking an already-revoked key returns `already_revoked: true`.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Merchant configuration. The markup set here is added on top of carrier rates and is the amount a customer is charged in the payment flow (computed server-side).

request.description: Read the merchant markup applied on top of carrier rates. `percentage_markup` is a percent (e.g. `12.0` = +12%); `fixed_fee_cents` is a flat add-on in cents; `fixed_fee_dollars` is the same value in dollars.
Update the merchant markup. Both fields required. Negative or non-numeric values return `400`. On the managed tier this markup on the shipping rate is how the service is funded.
